claimText: India signed a 15-year exploration contract with the International Seabed Authority (ISA) for exploring polymetallic sulphides in the Carlsberg Ridge.
evidenceExcerpt: India signed a 15-year contract with the ISA for exploring polymetallic sulphides in the Carlsberg Ridge, located in the Arabian Sea.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's deep-sea exploration contracts and locations.
distractorHooks: Contract signed for polymetallic nodules instead of sulphides
Contract signed for the Mid-Atlantic Ridge instead of the Carlsberg Ridge
Contract signed with UNEP instead of ISA

claimText: India holds an exploration contract for Polymetallic Nodules specifically in the Central Indian Ocean Basin.
evidenceExcerpt: India holds other exploration contracts for Polymetallic Nodules in the Central Indian Ocean Basin and PMS in the Indian Ocean Ridge.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching specific deep-sea resources to their respective ocean basins.
distractorHooks: Polymetallic Nodules in the Carlsberg Ridge
Polymetallic Nodules in the Pacific Ocean Basin

claimText: India's exploration approval for the Afanasy-Nikitin Sea (ANS) mount is pending because the territory is claimed by Sri Lanka.
evidenceExcerpt: India's exploration approval for the Afanasy-Nikitin Sea (ANS) mount is pending, as the territory is claimed by Sri Lanka.
examUse: Statement-based question on geopolitical disputes affecting deep-sea exploration in the Indian Ocean.

claimText: Polymetallic sulphides are rich in copper, zinc, gold, and silver, and are formed where hot, mineral-rich fluids from the Earth's mantle enter the ocean.
evidenceExcerpt: Polymetallic sulphides are rich in copper, zinc, gold, and silver, and form where hot, mineral-rich fluids from the Earth's mantle enter the ocean.
examUse: Statement-based question on the composition and geological formation of polymetallic sulphides.
distractorHooks: Rich primarily in uranium and thorium
Formed exclusively in shallow coastal waters

claimText: The International Seabed Authority (ISA) is an autonomous organization established in 1994 under UNCLOS, with its headquarters in Kingston, Jamaica.
evidenceExcerpt: The International Seabed Authority (ISA) is an autonomous organization established in 1994 under UNCLOS, headquartered in Kingston, Jamaica.
examUse: Institution-feature question testing the origin, parent framework, and headquarters of the ISA.
distractorHooks: Established under the UN Environment Programme (UNEP)

claimText: The Deep-Sea Biobank Initiative (DBI) 2025, AREA2030, and the Sustainable Seabed Knowledge Initiative (SSKI) are initiatives launched by the International Seabed Authority (ISA).
evidenceExcerpt: ISA initiatives include the Deep-Sea Biobank Initiative (DBI) 2025, AREA2030, and the Sustainable Seabed Knowledge Initiative (SSKI).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching global environmental/oceanic initiatives to their parent organizations.
distractorHooks: Initiatives of the International Maritime Organization (IMO)
Initiatives of the World Wildlife Fund (WWF)
Initiatives of the Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission (IOC)
